task_completion.description: Open the target spreadsheet in WPS Spreadsheet
task_completion.visual_evidence: WPS Spreadsheet is open and the file 'employee_sales.xlsx' is loaded and visible.

task_completion.description: Use employee names as rows in the pivot table
task_completion.visual_evidence: A pivot table is created with 'Employee Name' placed in the Rows area, displaying employee names down the first column.

task_completion.description: Show the sum of total sales by department and region
task_completion.visual_evidence: The pivot table includes 'Department' and 'Region' fields (likely in Columns or nested in Rows) and 'Sum of Sales' (or similar) in the Values area, showing aggregated totals.
The Values area of the pivot table includes a second metric for 'Average of Sales' (or similar), calculating the mean sales alongside the sum.

task_completion.description: Display grand totals in the pivot table
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Grand Total' row at the bottom and/or a 'Grand Total' column at the far right of the pivot table is visible.

task_completion.description: Apply professional styling to the table
task_completion.visual_evidence: The pivot table has a distinct visual style applied (e.g., colored headers, banded rows, or a specific PivotTable Style selected from the WPS Design ribbon).
The agent clicks the save icon, uses a save shortcut, or the file tab/title bar shows no unsaved indicator (like an asterisk) at the end of the task.

integrity.description: Agent used the WPS Spreadsheet GUI to create the pivot table
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the WPS Spreadsheet application interface, not a terminal running Python/Pandas scripts to modify the Excel file.

integrity.description: Agent created a real pivot table object
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'PivotTable Fields' pane or 'PivotTable Tools' ribbon is visible in the screenshots, proving it is a functional pivot table and not manually typed cells.

integrity.description: Agent used the actual data from the spreadsheet
integrity.visual_evidence: The pivot table fields match the actual dataset headers ('Department', 'Region', 'Employee Name') and the data is not hallucinated or manually typed from the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected pivot fields accurately match the headers ['Department', 'Region', 'Employee Name'] created in the spreadsheet. The data source is a 20-row modified subset of the Kaggle Superstore Sales dataset.
